title: The Bench That Says No
summary: A new bench in a Berlin park looks friendly, but it tells some people to go away.
body:
Clara walks in a small park in Berlin every morning. Last week the city put a new bench near the trees. It is clean and bright. It has three seats. But each seat has metal arms in the middle. Clara likes the arms. She can rest her bag on them. She thinks the bench is nice and modern.

One cold night, Clara sees an old man near the bench. His name is Anton. He has no home. He wants to lie down and sleep. But he cannot. The metal arms are in the way. He can only sit. Anton is tired and cold.

The next day Clara asks a park worker about the arms. The worker is honest. He says the arms are not only for bags. The city does not want people to sleep on the bench. The design looks kind, but it has a quiet rule. It says, "You can sit, but you cannot stay." Now Clara sees the same bench in a new way. The bench is still bright and clean. But it is not friendly to everyone.

annotations.meaningVi: luật ngầm, quy tắc không nói ra

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về thói quen hằng ngày. Thêm -s khi chủ ngữ là she/he/it.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for daily habits, as in "Clara walks in a small park in Berlin every morning."

grammarNotes.title: can / cannot for ability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'can' để nói có thể làm gì, và 'cannot' để nói không thể.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use can and cannot for ability: "He can only sit" and "But he cannot."

grammarNotes.title: but to show contrast
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'but' để nối hai ý trái ngược nhau.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: But links two opposite ideas, as in "The design looks kind, but it has a quiet rule."

translationVi:
Clara đi dạo trong một công viên nhỏ ở Berlin mỗi sáng. Tuần trước thành phố đặt một chiếc ghế dài mới gần hàng cây. Nó sạch và sáng. Nó có ba chỗ ngồi. Nhưng mỗi chỗ ngồi có tay vịn bằng kim loại ở giữa. Clara thích những tay vịn đó. Cô có thể đặt túi lên trên. Cô nghĩ chiếc ghế đẹp và hiện đại.

Một đêm lạnh, Clara thấy một ông già gần chiếc ghế. Tên ông là Anton. Ông không có nhà. Ông muốn nằm xuống và ngủ. Nhưng ông không thể. Những tay vịn kim loại cản đường. Ông chỉ có thể ngồi. Anton mệt và lạnh.

Hôm sau Clara hỏi một người làm trong công viên về các tay vịn. Người đó nói thật. Anh ấy nói rằng những tay vịn không chỉ để đặt túi. Thành phố không muốn người ta ngủ trên ghế. Thiết kế trông tử tế, nhưng nó có một luật ngầm. Nó nói: "Bạn có thể ngồi, nhưng bạn không thể ở lại." Bây giờ Clara nhìn chiếc ghế đó theo một cách mới. Chiếc ghế vẫn sáng và sạch. Nhưng nó không thân thiện với tất cả mọi người.

questions.prompt: Where does Clara walk every morning?
options: In a small park in Berlin
questions.explanation: The text says "Clara walks in a small park in Berlin every morning."

questions.prompt: How many seats does the new bench have?
questions.explanation: The passage says "It has three seats."

questions.prompt: Why does Clara like the metal arms at first?
options: She can rest her bag on them
questions.explanation: "She can rest her bag on them."

questions.prompt: What does Anton want to do at the bench?
questions.explanation: "He wants to lie down and sleep."

questions.prompt: Why can Anton not sleep on the bench?
options: The metal arms are in the way
questions.explanation: "The metal arms are in the way. He can only sit."

questions.prompt: Who tells Clara the truth about the arms?
questions.explanation: "Clara asks a park worker about the arms. The worker is honest."

questions.prompt: What is the real reason for the arms?
options: To stop people from sleeping there
questions.explanation: "The city does not want people to sleep on the bench."
questions.hint: Third paragraph, what the worker says.

questions.prompt: What does the phrase "a quiet rule" mean here?
options: A rule that is not spoken out loud
questions.explanation: The bench "has a quiet rule" — it sends a message without words.
questions.hint: Think about the meaning of "quiet" in this sentence.

questions.prompt: What does the bench seem to say to people?
options: You can sit, but you cannot stay
This seat is free for the night
questions.explanation: "It says, 'You can sit, but you cannot stay.'"

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: A nice-looking design can hide an unkind rule
questions.explanation: The bench "looks kind, but it has a quiet rule" and is "not friendly to everyone."

questions.prompt: How does Clara feel about the bench at the end?
options: She sees it in a new way
questions.explanation: "Now Clara sees the same bench in a new way."

questions.prompt: The word "honest" in the passage describes someone who...
questions.explanation: "The worker is honest. He says the arms are not only for bags."

blanks.explanation: "each seat has metal arms in the middle."
"She can rest her bag on them."
"He wants to lie down and sleep."
"The metal arms are in the way."
"Clara asks a park worker about the arms."
"The city does not want people to sleep on the bench."

criticalThinkingQuestion: The worker says the bench means "You can sit, but you cannot stay." Is it fair for a city to build public seats that only welcome some people? Why or why not?
